channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-3625] Test failure on test suite replay-single, subtest test_58c
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for gmahajan <gmahajan@ddn.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: http://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/10687878-f427-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c.

The sub-test test_58c failed with the following error:

test_58c failed with 2

Info required for matching: replay-single 58c
CMD: wtm-12vm3 lctl set_param fail_loc=0
fail_loc=0
getfattr: /mnt/lustre2/d0.replay-single/d58/f.replay-single.58c: No such file or directory
replay-single test_58c: @@@@@@ FAIL: test_58c failed with 2
Trace dump:
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4202:error_noexit()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4229:error()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4483:run_one()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4516:run_one_logged()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4371:run_test()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/replay-single.sh:1462:main()
Dumping lctl log to /logdir/test_logs/2013-07-23/lustre-reviews-el6-x86_64-review-zfs-1_3_1_16860_-70004232338460-021952/replay-single.test_58c.*.1374596486.log
item.summary: Test failure on test suite replay-single, subtest test_58c

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment: If it is no longer happening I think that it should be enough to close it as cannot repro and we can reopen the ticket if it is seen again.

The Maloo searching result shows that LU-3625 has disappeared since Aug.7 (probably fixed by some commit)? And the other issue on replay-single.sh test_58b/c(No such attribute) in LU-3795 was fixed in Sep.11.

Could we lower priority of this bug?
We would still like some investigation of the source of the problem even if the failure is only on ZFS. Did this start failing at a certain time, maybe related to some patch introducing a regression?
This failure for ldiskfs hasn't been seen in recent one month, but it still happens for zfs, so I add label "zfs".

I will use those 6 tar files as my sample data for repopulating the database tomorrow (2013 09 05). If it goes well then I will be trickling the data in for all the holes for the 6 weeks leading up to the maloo failure (2013 08 24).

The test sets mentioned in this ticket are:

  1. http://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/10687878-f427-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c is from test session e706d58e-f425-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c
  2. https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/c9919aca-f452-11e2-9221-52540035b04c is from test session da083cd4-f44c-11e2-9221-52540035b04c
  3. https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/560bf3da-f44c-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c is from test session 27b7e8dc-f44b-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c
  4. https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5d3ac8aa-f41c-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c is from test session ff470d9a-f41a-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c
  5. https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/66c76364-f03a-11e2-8ec3-52540035b04c is from test session 2262bfd6-f038-11e2-8ec3-52540035b04c
  6. https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e5a09d60-f899-11e2-a8b3-52540035b04c is from test session 51f42660-f897-11e2-a8b3-52540035b04c

These sessions’ log files have been recovered from the old corrupted maloo database, packed compressed tar files named test-session-id.tgz and put onto eric.whamcloud.com under ~mjs/for-jodi. Please let me know if you can see these and if they have the right content.

Sorry I am extracting at the test session rather that the test set, it will make it easier to repopulate the production database later.

mjs@eric:~/for-jodi$ ls -la
total 136960
drwxr-xr-x  2 mjs mjs     4096 2013-09-04 10:27 .
drwxr-xr-x 13 mjs mjs     4096 2013-09-04 04:41 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 18973284 2013-09-04 10:26 2262bfd6-f038-11e2-8ec3-52540035b04c.tgz
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 11392157 2013-09-04 10:26 27b7e8dc-f44b-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c.tgz
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 34412313 2013-09-04 10:26 51f42660-f897-11e2-a8b3-52540035b04c.tgz
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 41473228 2013-09-04 10:27 da083cd4-f44c-11e2-9221-52540035b04c.tgz
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 22509888 2013-09-04 10:27 e706d58e-f425-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c.tgz
-rw-r--r--  1 mjs mjs 11441855 2013-09-04 10:27 ff470d9a-f41a-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c.tgz

There seems to be a bit of this going on:

CMD: wtm-23vm6.rosso.whamcloud.com setfattr -n trusted.foo -v  /mnt/lustre/d0.replay-single/d58/f.replay-single.58c
setfattr: /mnt/lustre/d0.replay-single/d58/f.replay-single.58c: No such attribute
CMD: wtm-23vm3 lctl set_param fail_loc=0

That just looks plain broken to me bit it is was the test wants.

As of today a very high failure rate. 

test_58c 	

    Error: 'test_58c failed with 2'
    Failure Rate: 33.00% of last 100 executions [all branches]

Several instances of this failure for ldiskfs and zfs. Here are just a few:
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/c9919aca-f452-11e2-9221-52540035b04c
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/560bf3da-f44c-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5d3ac8aa-f41c-11e2-b8a2-52540035b04c
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/66c76364-f03a-11e2-8ec3-52540035b04c

From dmesg, the mount fails:

Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == replay-single test 58c: resend/reconstruct setxattr op == 13:29:38 (1374611378)
Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mkdir -p /mnt/lustre2
Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,acl,flock wtm-11vm7@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre2
LustreError: 152-6: Ignoring deprecated mount option 'acl'.
LustreError: 15c-8: MGC10.10.16.129@tcp: The configuration from log 'lustre-client' failed (-5). This may be the result of communication errors between this node and the MGS, a bad configuration, or other errors. See the syslog for more information.
LustreError: 26123:0:(llite_lib.c:1036:ll_fill_super()) Unable to process log: -5
Lustre: Unmounted lustre-client
LustreError: 26123:0:(obd_mount.c:1277:lustre_fill_super()) Unable to mount  (-5)
Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: setfattr -n trusted.foo -v bar /mnt/lustre/d0.replay-single/d58/f.replay-single.58c

From debug_log:

00010000:00010000:0.0:1374611379.318674:0:26123:0:(ldlm_request.c:1133:ldlm_cli_cancel_local()) not sending request (at caller's instruction)
00010000:00010000:0.0:1374611379.318676:0:26123:0:(ldlm_lockd.c:1718:ldlm_handle_bl_callback()) ### client blocking callback handler END ns: MGC10.10.16.129@tcp lock: ffff88007acfe1c0/0x9fa72e6c99f6685e lrc: 3/0,0 mode: --/CR res: [0x65727473756c:0x0:0x0].0 rrc: 4 type: PLN flags: 0x5a0f400000000 nid: local remote: 0x0 expref: -99 pid: 26123 timeout: 0 lvb_type: 0
00010000:00010000:0.0:1374611379.318678:0:26123:0:(ldlm_lock.c:219:ldlm_lock_put()) ### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.10.16.129@tcp lock: ffff88007acfe1c0/0x9fa72e6c99f6685e lrc: 0/0,0 mode: --/CR res: [0x65727473756c:0x0:0x0].0 rrc: 4 type: PLN flags: 0x5a0f400000000 nid: local remote: 0x0 expref: -99 pid: 26123 timeout: 0 lvb_type: 0
10000000:01000000:0.0:1374611379.318684:0:26123:0:(mgc_request.c:1801:mgc_process_log()) Can't get cfg lock: -14
10000000:01000000:0.0:1374611379.318690:0:26123:0:(mgc_request.c:1820:mgc_process_log()) MGC10.10.16.129@tcp: configuration from log 'lustre-client' failed (-5).
00000020:02020000:0.0:1374611379.318692:0:26123:0:(obd_mount.c:118:lustre_process_log()) 15c-8: MGC10.10.16.129@tcp: The configuration from log 'lustre-client' failed (-5). This may be the result of communication errors between this node and the MGS, a bad configuration, or other errors. See the syslog for more information.
00000080:00020000:0.0:1374611379.320502:0:26123:0:(llite_lib.c:1036:ll_fill_super()) Unable to process log: -5
00000080:00200000:0.0:1374611379.321126:0:26123:0:(llite_lib.c:1091:ll_put_super()) VFS Op: sb ffff88005cc7f000 - lustre-client
10000000:01000000:0.0:1374611379.321207:0:26123:0:(mgc_request.c:140:config_log_put()) dropping config log lustre-cliir
10000000:01000000:0.0:1374611379.321209:0:26123:0:(mgc_request.c:405:config_log_end()) end config log lustre-client (0)
00000080:02000400:0.0:1374611379.321291:0:26123:0:(llite_lib.c:1125:ll_put_super()) Unmounted lustre-client
00000020:01000000:0.0:1374611379.321538:0:26123:0:(obd_config.c:911:class_del_profile()) Del profile lustre-client
